mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for two consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance increases borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% approaching restrictive threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% for 2+ consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of months following PAYEMS month-over-month declines, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with declining real retail sales (RSAFS adjusted for inflation) signals eroding consumer purchasing power and demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increase > 0.3% AND RSAFS month-over-month decline > 1%
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 320.321 and RSAFS at 724131.0 million dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (both conditions met) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply-side weakness and reduced corporate capacity utilization, compressing profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: supply-constrained contraction or post-shock recovery failure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances following 2-month INDPRO declines, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% month-over-month, signaling a sharp contraction in residential investment demand and forward-looking economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations.
regime_hint: demand shock or monetary tightening transmission to housing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following HOUST declines > 10%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0%, signaling both labor market deterioration and persistent monetary restriction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.2% and FEDFUNDS at 4.33%
regime_hint: monetary tightening with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, signaling late-cycle weakness and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn negative.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 23526.085 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of quarters following GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates by more than 0.5 percentage points month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains elevated above 4.0%, signaling disinflation progress that increases the probability of near-term rate cuts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change decelerates > 0.5pp AND FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 320.321 and FEDFUNDS at 4.33%
regime_hint: disinflation cycle with monetary policy pivot expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
